---
title: "Spatial Transcriptomics"
date: 2026-05-18
categories: [method, spatial-omics]
tags: [spatial-transcriptomics, gene-expression, tissue-organization, spatial-biology]
draft: false
---

## Definition

Spatial transcriptomics measures gene expression while preserving information about where transcripts, cells, or spots are located in tissue.

## What It Gives

- Gene expression with spatial coordinates
- Tissue-region-specific expression programs
- Spatial gradients from tumour core to stroma
- Input data for spatial domain and spatial ecotype analysis

## Common Platforms

- 10x Genomics Visium
- Visium HD
- MERSCOPE
- Xenium
- Legacy ST platforms

## Main Limitation

Platforms trade off spatial resolution, gene coverage, throughput, cost, and sensitivity. Some assays capture transcriptome-wide data at spot-level resolution; others provide single-cell resolution with targeted gene panels.

## In Papers

- Zhang et al. 2026 integrates multiple spatial transcriptomics platforms to identify and validate spatial ecotypes.
